heading: Authorization of appropriations; payment of salaries and expenses
label: 289b Authorization of appropriations; payment of salaries and expenses
notes: References in TextSubchapter XVI [§ 287 et seq.] of this chapter, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), was in the original a reference to the United Nations Participation Act of 1945.

CodificationIn subsec. (b)(1), “chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5” substituted for “the Classification Act of 1949” on authority of , , , the first section of which enacted Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.Pub. L. 89–554, § 7(b)80 Stat. 631Sept. 6, 1966

children.label: a
children.text: such sums, not to exceed $73,325,000 for the fiscal year beginning , as may be necessary for the payment of United States contributions to the Organization (consisting of supplies, services, or funds and all necessary expenses related thereto) as determined in accordance with article 10 of the constitution of the Organization; andJuly 1, 1947

salaries of the representative or representatives and alternates provided for in , and appropriate staff, including personal services in the District of Columbia and elsewhere, without regard to the civil-service laws and chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5; andsection 289a of this title

such other expenses as the Secretary of State deems necessary to participation by the United States in the activities of the Organization; , That the provisions of and regulations thereunder, applicable to expenses incurred pursuant to subchapter XVI of this chapter shall be applicable to any expenses incurred pursuant to this chapter.Providedsection 287e of this title
